message:
Quarantine freed pages until commit to prevent cross-tree page aliasing

Previously, allocate_page() would immediately reuse pages freed earlier
in the same transaction via freed_pages.pop(). This created a subtle
correctness hazard: if one B-tree operation freed a page and another
B-tree operation in the same transaction then allocated it, the page
could end up owned by two different trees before the original ownership
was durably retired. On crash recovery, this cross-tree page aliasing
could manifest as data corruption.

The fix removes the immediate freed-page reuse path. Freed pages now
stay quarantined for the duration of the transaction and only re-enter
the allocator's freelist after the transaction commits successfully.
New allocations during the same transaction extend the database file
instead, which is safe because the extra pages get reclaimed on the
next transaction.

Adds test_freed_pages_are_quarantined_until_commit() which verifies:
- A page freed mid-transaction is NOT reused by the next allocate_page()
  in that same transaction (allocation extends the file instead)
- After commit, the freed page IS reused by allocate_page() in a
  subsequent transaction (confirming it re-enters the freelist)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
